name: zcf
description: Zero-Config Code Flow for Claude code & Codex

commit.message:
chore: release v3.6.3

- Update version to 3.6.3
- Update CHANGELOG.md
- Generated by /zcf-release command

Made-with: Cursor

feat(bmad): add multiple agent commands and workflows

- Introduced new agent commands for various roles including 'bmad-master', 'analyst', 'architect', 'dev', 'pm', 'qa', 'sm', 'tech-writer', 'ux-designer', and more.
- Each command includes detailed activation instructions and descriptions for their respective functionalities.
- Updated .gitignore to exclude BMad Method V6 related directories.

feat(sponsorship): add Crazyrouter as a new sponsor in documentation files

- Updated README files in English, Japanese, and Chinese to include Crazyrouter sponsorship details.
- Added Crazyrouter to the list of API provider presets in configuration.
- Introduced documentation for Crazyrouter's features and usage examples in relevant sections.
chore: update GLM-en.png asset

chore: release v3.6.2

- Update version to 3.6.2
- Update CHANGELOG.md
- Generated by /zcf-release command

Made-with: Cursor
feat(settings): expand permissions in settings.json
docs(mcp): update DeepWiki documentation link in English and Chinese versions
feat(api-providers): update API provider presets and add new services
docs: update sponsorship details in README and documentation files

fix(codex-backup): address review feedback from Codex

- P1: Use precise path matching for tmp dir exclusion (avoid false positives)
- P2: Preserve valid symlinks instead of skipping all (for dotfiles setups)
- Only skip dangling symlinks (target does not exist)
- Add tests for symlink handling (valid symlinks, symlinked dirs)

fix(codex-backup): skip tmp dir and handle dangling symlinks

- Exclude /tmp directory from Codex backup (runtime artifacts, not needed)
- Use lstatSync instead of statSync to avoid following symlinks
- Skip symlinks entirely during backup (typically runtime artifacts)
- Handle ENOENT errors gracefully (files disappearing during traversal)
- Update tests to mock lstatSync with isSymbolicLink method

Fixes #319 - ENOENT error when backing up ~/.codex/tmp/path/codex-*/apply_patch
(dangling symlink from Codex runtime)

fix(output-style): address review feedback from Qodo/Cursor/Codex

- Fix sort comparator: handle both a and b being "default" for deterministic ordering
- Fix cancel handling: distinguish undefined (cancel) from "__none__" (explicit choice)
- Fix persistence: use "none" marker instead of empty string to avoid || fallback
- Fix zcf-config.ts: use ?? instead of || for defaultOutputStyle to preserve falsy values
- Update tests to match new "none" marker value

feat(output-style): allow skipping output style selection

- Remove mandatory validation on custom style checkbox, allow empty selection
- Add "none" option to skip output style entirely and preserve Claude Code defaults
- Add clearGlobalOutputStyle() to remove outputStyle key from settings.json
- Reorder style list: default first, custom styles grouped, built-in styles last
- Sort built-in styles with "default" prioritized in default style selection
- Add i18n keys for noOutputStyle/noOutputStyleDesc/outputStyleCleared (en + zh-CN)
- Add unit tests for clearGlobalOutputStyle and empty selection scenarios

Closes #313

feat(bmad): upgrade bmad-init template from V4 to V6

- Replace expect-based interactive install with non-interactive --yes flag
- Add {output_folder} → _bmad-output rename workaround (v6.0.0-Beta.8 bug)
- Add automatic .gitignore migration (clean V4 entries, add V6 entries)
- Add legacy V4 installation detection and migration guidance
- Update post-install instructions with new V6 commands (/bmad-help, /bmad-bmm-*)
- Sync English and Chinese versions with locale-specific language params

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: release v3.6.1

- Update version to 3.6.1
- Update CHANGELOG.md

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(api-providers): add AICodeMirror API provider presets

Add two new API provider presets for AICodeMirror service:
- aicodemirror: Global High-Quality Line
- aicodemirror-cn: China Optimized Line

Both presets support Claude Code and Codex with auth_token authentication.

Changes:
- Add provider configurations in src/config/api-providers.ts
- Add test cases for new providers
- Update trilingual documentation (zh-CN, en, ja-JP)

feat(codex): remove support for Codex chat format

- Eliminated chat format support from Codex, updating related configurations to default to 'responses'.
- Modified multiple files including api-providers.ts, codex-provider-manager.ts, and codex-config-switch.ts to reflect the removal of chat options.
- Updated internationalization files to remove chat-related translations and adjusted validation logic accordingly.
- Ensured all relevant tests were updated to validate the new configuration.

docs: add AICodeMirror sponsorship details to README and documentation files

- Included AICodeMirror as a new sponsor in the English, Japanese, and Chinese README and documentation files.
- Added promotional details and benefits for users registering through AICodeMirror's link.

chore: release v3.6.0

- Update version to 3.6.0
- Update CHANGELOG.md

🤖 Generated by /zcf-release command

Revert "docs: add DartNode badge to README and documentation files"

This reverts commit 6b452b3da1bdbd601323f1f4f127652071747dc4.

feat: add Leibus engineer output style (#296)

- Add leibus-engineer output style with presentation-style personality
- Add Chinese and English translations for the new style
- Create bilingual template files with comprehensive behavior guidelines
- Update test cases to reflect new style count (8 total, 5 custom)

The Leibus engineer style combines:
- Engineering rigor with marketing talent
- Product thinking and data-driven communication
- Internet-style rapid iteration mindset
- "Are you OK?" humor approachable to users

* test: update output styles count and add Leibus engineer style

* feat: add 'leibus-engineer' output style and update test cases

* chore:remove .claude/settings.local.json

---------

Co-authored-by: 苗大 <ufo025174@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix(codex): enhance provider name validation in configureCodexApi

- Updated the provider name validation logic to allow uppercase letters in display names while normalizing to lowercase for IDs.
- Improved the rejection criteria to only disallow truly invalid characters, enhancing user experience and flexibility in provider name input.

docs: add DartNode badge to README and documentation files

- Included a new badge for "Powered by DartNode" in the English, Japanese, and Chinese README and documentation files.
- Updated the layout of existing badges for consistency across all language versions.
docs: add 'rem-engineer' output style to multiple language files
feat: add 'rem-engineer' output style and update related configurations

chore: release v3.5.1

- Update version to 3.5.1
- Update CHANGELOG.md with patch changes
- Generated by /zcf-release command

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: remove unused configuration and memory files

- Deleted obsolete .gitignore, project.yml, and memory files related to API refactor plans and project overview.
- Cleaned up the repository by removing files that are no longer necessary for the current project structure.

Refs: #259

feat(codex): update Codex model options and default configuration

- Introduced new Codex model options: `gpt-5.1-codex-max`, `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`, and updated default to `gpt-5.2`.
- Modified configuration prompts and logic across multiple files to reflect new model options.
- Updated English and Chinese translation files to include descriptions for new models.
- Ensured backward compatibility by adjusting default values and choices in user prompts.

Refs: #259

fix(toml): improve newline handling and optional field insertion in zcf-config

- Ensure content is always inserted on a new line after the version field in the insertAfterVersionField function.
- Update the writeTomlConfig function to conditionally include optional Claude Code fields only if they are defined, preventing unnecessary edits and potential issues with batch editing.

Refs: #259

refactor(codex): remove writeCodexConfig function

- Removed the writeCodexConfig function to streamline configuration handling.
- Updated tests to replace calls to writeCodexConfig with batchUpdateCodexMcpServices, ensuring proper functionality and integration.
- Enhanced mocking in tests to reflect the removal of writeCodexConfig, maintaining test coverage for configuration operations.

Refs: #259

fix(toml): implement targeted TOML updates to prevent MCP configuration corruption

- Introduced a new utility module `codex-toml-updater` for precise modifications to TOML files, ensuring that changes to API configurations do not affect MCP configurations.
- Updated multiple functions across the codebase to utilize targeted updates, preserving existing MCP services and other configurations.
- Added a new markdown file documenting the issue and solution for reference.
- Enhanced tests to validate the integrity of configurations after updates.

Refs: #259

fix(toml): enhance regex for top-level TOML fields to support inline comments

- Update regex patterns in insertAfterVersionField and updateTopLevelTomlFields functions to handle inline comments for version and lastUpdated fields.
- Add tests to ensure correct handling of TOML fields with inline comments and trailing spaces.
- Improve overall robustness of TOML configuration handling.

Refs: #277

fix(toml): fix top-level field handling in updateTopLevelTomlFields

- add section boundary detection to prevent modifying fields inside sections
- fix newline handling when inserting lastUpdated after version field
- add insertAtTopLevelStart helper for proper comment preservation
- add insertAfterVersionField helper for correct field positioning
- add comprehensive test cases for edge scenarios (PR #277 bugs)

Refs: #277
docs: move legacy plan markdown files to .zcf/plan/history

refactor(toml): replace smol-toml with @rainbowatcher/toml-edit-js

- add new toml-edit utility module with format-preserving editing
- implement incremental TOML editing in zcf-config to preserve user customizations
- replace smol-toml imports across the codebase
- add comprehensive tests for toml-edit functionality
- update documentation for new TOML parsing approach

docs: update sponsor sections in README files

- Add UUCode as a new corporate sponsor with $100 proxy credits in English, Japanese, and Chinese README files
- Organize sponsors into distinct corporate and individual categories for clarity
- Ensure consistency across all language versions of the documentation

docs: fix output style command across multiple languages

- Replace `/set-output-style` with `/output-style` in documentation
- Affected files: templates.md and output-styles.md in English, Japanese, and Chinese versions
- Ensure consistency in command usage for better user experience

docs: update Haleclipse image and remove inline styles

- Update Haleclipse.webp image asset (1600x656)
- Remove hardcoded width="200" and border-radius inline styles
- Apply changes across en, zh-CN, and ja-JP documentation
- Affected files: templates.md and output-styles.md in all locales

chore(config): update GLM default model to GLM-4.7

- Update Codex default model from GLM-4.6 to GLM-4.7
- Sync documentation with new model version
- Update README files (en, zh-CN, ja-JP) with latest model info

chore: release v3.5.0

- Update version to 3.5.0
- Update CHANGELOG.md with bilingual release notes
- Consolidate templates into common directory
- Fix CCR proxy configuration
- Enhance sixStep workflow

🤖 Generated by /zcf-release command

chore: update docs, assets and remove deprecated memory files

- remove deprecated .cunzhi-memory directory
- update TopBanner promotion from GLM-4.6 to GLM-4.7 with cross-year discount
- optimize GLM asset images (reduced file sizes)
- update CLAUDE.md to reflect v3.4.3 and consolidated template architecture
- update workflow docs to use unified .zcf/plan/ directory structure across all locales

docs(workflow): add user approval request in sixStep workflow Phase 2

- include a new step to request user approval before proceeding in both English and Chinese versions
- enhance clarity and ensure user engagement in the workflow process

docs(workflow): update archiving filename format in sixStep workflow

- modify the archiving filename format to include `[Finish Time]` followed by the task name
- specify the finish time format as `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS` for consistency in both English and Chinese versions

docs(workflow): add timestamp acquisition rules to sixStep workflow

- add mandatory bash command requirement for accurate timestamp retrieval
- provide common date format command examples (default, filename, readable, ISO)
- document typical use cases including document updates and plan archiving

docs(workflow): reorder execution phase steps in sixStep workflow

- move plan storage step before user approval requirement
- improve workflow clarity and logical flow in both en and zh-CN versions

refactor(templates): enhance sixStep workflow plan directory structure

- introduce hierarchical plan directory with current/ and history/ subdirectories
- update plan file paths from `.zcf/plan/task-name.md` to `.zcf/plan/current/task-name.md`
- add task archiving mechanism to move completed tasks to history/ directory
- update both Chinese and English workflow template versions consistently
- include planning documentation for path structure refactoring tasks

refactor(templates): unify sixStep plan directory to .zcf

- replace $CONFIG_DIR variable with .zcf in sixStep workflow templates
- remove template variable processing logic from Claude Code installer
- remove template variable processing logic from Codex installer
- update workflow-installer tests to reflect simplified copy logic
- update codex tests to remove variable replacement validation
- update templates documentation to reflect unified .zcf directory

This change simplifies the codebase by eliminating the need for runtime
variable replacement, making both Claude Code and Codex use the same
.zcf directory for plan files.

test(codex): add unit tests for runCodexWorkflowSelection presetWorkflows filtering

- add test for empty presetWorkflows array (install all workflows)
- add test for valid workflow names filtering
- add test for invalid workflow names filtering
- add test for Git grouped workflow expansion
- add test for multiple workflows installation
- add test for template variable processing ($CONFIG_DIR -> .codex)
- add test for all invalid workflows scenario
- add test for English locale support
- fix i18n mock to include gitWorkflow translation

docs: add template consolidation refactoring plans

- add git workflow templates consolidation plan
- add output-styles templates consolidation plan
- add sixStep workflow templates consolidation plan
- all plans aim to eliminate duplication and follow DRY principle
- target structure uses shared templates/common/ directory

test: verify template consolidation to common directory

- add tests for output-styles using shared common/output-styles path
- add tests for git and sixStep workflows using shared common/workflow path
- add comprehensive Codex common templates test suite
- verify template variable processing ($CONFIG_DIR -> .claude)
- ensure both Claude Code and Codex use consolidated template structure

fix(codex): correct template path structure after consolidation

- update workflow path from templates/common/workflow/{lang} to templates/common/workflow/{category}/{lang}
- implement language fallback mechanism when English templates are unavailable
- update function signatures to pass preferredLang parameter
- fix test assertions to match new common template structure
- add curly brackets to comply with ESLint antfu/curly rule

Fixes template path resolution after PR #260 template consolidation

docs: update CLAUDE.md files to reflect template consolidation

- update src/utils/CLAUDE.md to reflect new common template architecture
- update src/utils/code-tools/CLAUDE.md to reflect Codex template changes
- update templates/CLAUDE.md to document new common template structure

refactor(templates): consolidate templates into common directory

- migrate output-styles from claude-code/codex to common/output-styles
- migrate git workflow templates to common/workflow/git
- migrate sixStep workflow templates to common/workflow/sixStep
- update template loading logic in output-style.ts and workflow-installer.ts
- update codex.ts to reference new common template structure

fix(ccr): remove ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN when configuring CCR proxy

  Remove ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from settings.json when switching to CCR
  proxy mode to avoid configuration conflicts between official auth
  and CCR proxy settings.

chore: update release workflow configuration

- Replace NODE_AUTH_TOKEN with NPM_CONFIG_PROVENANCE in release.yml to enhance security and provenance tracking during package releases.

chore: release v3.4.3

- Update version to 3.4.3
- Update CHANGELOG.md with bilingual release notes

🤖 Generated by /zcf-release command

fix(updater): add sudo support for Linux non-root users

- Migrate from child_process to tinyexec for command execution
- Add shouldUseSudoForGlobalInstall() detection for Linux environments
- Apply sudo prefix to npm update commands (CCR, CometixLine) and
  claude update for non-root Linux users
- Homebrew installations continue to use brew without sudo
- Add i18n messages for sudo usage notification (en/zh-CN)
- Update tests to use tinyexec mock and add comprehensive sudo coverage

Co-authored-by: LoopOuroboros <36392404+LoopOuroboros@users.noreply.github.com>

test: improve coverage for skip-prompt mode and multi-config handling

Add tests for:
- configureCodexMcp with mcpServices=false (skip MCP entirely)
- configureCodexMcp with specific mcpServices array
- configureCodexMcp with workflows option passed through
- handleMultiConfigurations error handling when provider add fails
- handleMultiConfigurations using provider as fallback for name

These tests improve patch coverage for:
- src/utils/code-tools/codex-configure.ts (73% -> 86%)
- src/commands/init.ts (multi-config handling paths)

fix(codex): respect workflows and mcpServices options in skip-prompt mode

Addresses P1 review comments:

1. Skip-prompt workflow selection flags ignored:
   - Changed `runCodexWorkflowSelection({ skipPrompt: true, workflows: [] })`
   - To `runCodexWorkflowSelection({ skipPrompt: true, workflows: options?.workflows ?? [] })`
   - Now respects --workflows option in non-interactive mode

2. Skip-prompt MCP service selection overridden:
   - Added `mcpServices?: string[] | false` to CodexFullInitOptions interface
   - If mcpServices is false, skip MCP installation entirely
   - If mcpServices is an array, use that list instead of default services
   - Now respects --mcp-services=skip and custom service lists

fix(tests): update tests to match PR #251 behavior changes

- codex-provider-manager.test.ts: Update test to expect successful config creation when none exists (previously expected error)
- codex-incremental-config.test.ts: Update test to match new addProviderToExisting behavior
- init-multi-config.test.ts: Update expected provider ID to lowercase (matching new ID generation logic)
- codex-language-selection.test.ts: Add missing platform mock functions (normalizeTomlPath, getSystemRoot) and remove unreliable prompts assertion
- codex.test.ts: Simplify skip-prompt test to test actual verifiable behavior without relying on vi.spyOn for internal function calls

refactor(codex): optimize skip-prompt mode initialization flow

- Add backup caching mechanism to prevent duplicate backups during
  skip-prompt initialization
- Enhance multi-config provider handling by skipping built-in API setup
  when apiConfigs present
- Fix provider ID generation to use consistent naming convention across
  init flow
- Improve codex-configure to auto-install default MCP services in
  skip-prompt mode
- Update codex-provider-manager to support adding providers without
  existing configuration
- Preserve existing configuration data (mcpServices, otherConfig) during
  API setup
- Set default wire_api to 'responses' for consistent API compatibility
- Add comprehensive test coverage for skip-prompt scenarios and config
  preservation

This refactoring improves the non-interactive initialization experience by:
- Reducing redundant file operations through smart backup caching
- Ensuring seamless multi-provider configuration without manual API setup
- Maintaining backward compatibility with existing configurations
- Providing better defaults for new installations

BREAKING CHANGE: Custom API configurations now default to
wire_api='responses' instead of 'chat' for better compatibility with
standard API endpoints

refactor(profile): enhance model handling in profile management

- Updated the `handleAddProfile` function to support additional model fields for Sonnet and Opus, ensuring comprehensive model assignment from prefilled defaults.
- Refined the `updateDefaultModel` function to maintain environment variables when using custom models and to provide a fallback to default when model values are invalid.
- Enhanced unit tests to validate the new model handling logic, ensuring that profiles correctly apply the four-model architecture during configuration.

These changes improve the flexibility and robustness of profile management, aligning with the recent updates to the model configuration system.

refactor(claude-code): expose getAuthTypeLabel function and enhance tests

- Changed the visibility of the `getAuthTypeLabel` function to export, allowing it to be used in other modules.
- Updated unit tests to cover the new function, ensuring it correctly maps known authentication types to i18n keys and handles unknown types gracefully.
- Removed redundant model-related environment variable clearing in the API configuration process to streamline settings management.
- Added tests to validate the preservation of custom environment variables during API credential rotation.

These changes improve the modularity of the code and enhance the robustness of the configuration and validation processes.

docs(config): enhance API model configuration with four-model architecture

Expanded the API model configuration to a four-model architecture, allowing for more granular control over model selection. The new structure includes distinct parameters for Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus models, replacing the previous fast model parameter. Updated CLI options and configuration examples to reflect these changes, ensuring backward compatibility with existing configurations.

Key Changes:
- Introduced `apiHaikuModel`, `apiSonnetModel`, and `apiOpusModel` parameters.
- Updated CLI commands to use new model flags.
- Enhanced documentation to guide users through the new configuration process.

This refactor improves the flexibility and usability of the API model settings, aligning with the latest model offerings.

refactor(config): migrate from dual-model to four-model API configuration

Upgrade the API model configuration system from a two-model architecture
(primary + fast) to a four-model architecture (primary + haiku + sonnet + opus)
to provide more granular model selection and better align with Anthropic's
model family structure.

## Key Changes

- Replace `apiFastModel`/`fastModel` parameters with three distinct model params:
  - `apiHaikuModel`/`defaultHaikuModel` (maps to ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL)
  - `apiSonnetModel`/`defaultSonnetModel` (maps to ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL)
  - `apiOpusModel`/`defaultOpusModel` (maps to ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL)
- Update `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` to represent the default/primary model
- Introduce `clearModelEnv()` function in new `config.model-keys.ts` module to
  ensure clean model environment variable management
- Update all CLI options from `-F/--api-fast-model` to three separate flags:
  `-H/--api-haiku-model`, `-S/--api-sonnet-model`, `-O/--api-opus-model`
- Enhance `ClaudeCodeConfigManager` to properly handle all four model fields
  when switching profiles
- Update parameter validation to use generic error messages for model params

## i18n Updates

- Replace translation keys for model configuration across en/zh-CN locales:
  - `apiFastModel` → `apiHaikuModel`, `apiSonnetModel`, `apiOpusModel`
  - `enterFastModel` → `enterHaikuModel`, `enterSonnetModel`, `enterOpusModel`
  - `invalidApiFastModel` → generic `invalidModelParam` with dynamic key

## Type System Changes

- Update `ClaudeCodeProfile` interface with new model fields
- Modify `ApiConfigDefinition` to support four-model structure
- Ensure backward compatibility by gracefully handling missing model fields

## Testing Updates

- Update 8 test files to reflect new four-model parameter structure
- Simplify `config.test.ts` by removing redundant fast model clearing tests
- Update validation tests to cover all four model parameters

## Migration Impact

- Existing configurations with `fastModel` will continue to work
- `ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL` is now deprecated but still cleaned during config switch
- No breaking changes for users; new fields are optional

## Technical Details

- Centralized model environment variable keys in `MODEL_ENV_KEYS` constant
- Applied `clearModelEnv()` in both `config.ts` and `claude-code-config-manager.ts`
- Updated provider preset system to support four-model array structure
- Enhanced incremental profile management for copy/edit/add operations

Closes #N/A (internal refactor)

refactor(git): enhance commit message body and footer guidelines

- add detailed body format rules with imperative verb requirements
- prohibit colon-separated formats in commit body items
- add footer section guidelines for BREAKING CHANGE and git trailers
- wrap example code blocks with proper markdown fencing
- add complete examples with body and breaking change scenarios

chore: release v3.4.2

- Update version to 3.4.2
- Update CHANGELOG.md
- Generated by /zcf-release command

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

refactor(codex): enhance backup message handling and env_key migration logic

- Updated the `getBackupMessage` function to provide a fallback message when i18n is not initialized, preventing migration failures during early calls.
- Introduced a new `migrateEnvKeyInContent` function to handle the migration of env_key to temp_env_key, ensuring no duplicates are created in TOML sections with mixed states.
- Enhanced unit tests to cover new scenarios for both backup message retrieval and env_key migration, ensuring comprehensive validation of the changes.

These improvements enhance the robustness of the backup messaging and migration processes, ensuring smoother operations in various states.

refactor(codex): update env_key migration logic to ensure comprehensive migration

- Modified the `needsEnvKeyMigration` function to return true if any `env_key` entries exist, regardless of the presence of `temp_env_key`, ensuring all providers are migrated.
- Updated related tests to reflect the new migration logic, including scenarios where both `env_key` and `temp_env_key` are present, confirming that migration is still required.
- Added a new test case to verify that all `env_key` entries are migrated to `temp_env_key`, enhancing the robustness of the migration process.

This change improves the migration handling by ensuring no legacy keys are left unaddressed.

refactor(codex): change startup timeout from milliseconds to seconds

- Updated the Codex configuration to replace `startup_timeout_ms` with `startup_timeout_sec` for improved clarity and consistency.
- Adjusted related parsing, rendering, and testing logic to accommodate the new timeout format.
- Ensured all tests reflect the updated configuration structure, verifying the correct handling of the new timeout field.

This change enhances the readability of the configuration and aligns with common practices for specifying timeouts.

refactor(config): migrate env_key to temp_env_key across the codebase

- Updated all instances of `envKey` to `tempEnvKey` in configuration files and related logic to support the migration.
- Implemented functions to check and perform the migration from `env_key` to `temp_env_key` in the Codex configuration.
- Enhanced localization files to include messages related to the migration completion.
- Updated tests to reflect changes in the configuration structure and ensure proper functionality.

This refactor improves the consistency of environment key handling and prepares the codebase for future enhancements.

refactor(i18n): enhance localization for installation and common messages

- Added new localization keys for English and Chinese, including messages related to duplicate installations and installation sources.
- Updated existing keys for better clarity and consistency in user feedback during installation processes.
- Improved handling of symlink creation messages to ensure users receive accurate information based on their installation method.

This update enhances the user experience by providing clearer guidance and support for different installation scenarios.

refactor(installer): enhance installation verification and symlink handling

- Added functionality to verify installation and ensure symlink creation for Claude Code, addressing cases where installations may be missing or broken.
- Updated the verification process to provide user feedback on installation status, including success and error messages.
- Improved symlink handling logic to account for Homebrew cask installations, ensuring a smoother user experience.

This update enhances the reliability of the installation process and provides clearer feedback to users regarding their installation status.

refactor(installer): simplify installation verification and symlink creation logic

- Removed unnecessary dependency on dirname in the installer utility.
- Updated the installation method to always return true, indicating success regardless of verification results.
- Enhanced symlink creation logic to check for the existence of the bin directory directly, ensuring symlinks are only created in valid locations.

This refactor streamlines the installation process and improves user experience by providing consistent success feedback.

feat(installer): enhance installation process with verification and symlink creation

- Added new installation methods for npm, Homebrew, curl, PowerShell, and cmd in localization files.
- Implemented installation verification to check command availability and create symlinks if necessary.
- Updated installer functions to handle EEXIST errors during npm installations by using the --force flag.
- Improved user feedback during installation and verification processes with appropriate messages.

This update enhances the installation experience by ensuring successful command execution and providing clear guidance for users.

feat(updater): enhance update logic for Claude Code to support Homebrew installations

- Updated the update process to detect if Claude Code is installed via Homebrew and adjust the update command accordingly.
- Introduced new functions to check installation method and retrieve the latest version from Homebrew.
- Improved version checking logic to accommodate both Homebrew and npm installations, ensuring accurate update detection.

This change enhances the flexibility and reliability of the update process for users with different installation methods.

fix(docs): enhance TopBanner component with reactive language support

- Updated the TopBanner component to use computed properties for dynamic content based on the user's language selection.
- Introduced a computed close button aria-label for improved accessibility.
- Refactored language handling logic to enhance maintainability and responsiveness.

This update improves the user experience by ensuring the banner content and close button are accurately reflected in the selected language.

feat(docs): enhance documentation with UnoCSS integration and new components

- Added UnoCSS configuration to the VitePress setup for improved styling capabilities.
- Introduced a TopBanner component to display promotional content dynamically based on the user's language.
- Updated the documentation structure to include new dependencies and configurations for better user experience.
- Created a new TypeScript configuration file for the documentation to ensure proper type checking and module resolution.
- Enhanced the overall layout and styling of the documentation site with custom CSS adjustments.

This update significantly improves the visual appeal and functionality of the documentation site, making it more engaging for users.

chore: release v3.4.1

- Update version to 3.4.1
- Update CHANGELOG.md
- Generated by /zcf-release command

feat(docs): add favicon to site configuration

- Added a favicon link to the VitePress configuration for improved branding and user experience.
- Introduced a new favicon file in the assets directory to be used across the documentation site.

This update enhances the visual identity of the documentation site by incorporating a favicon.

fix(codex): update Homebrew cask version retrieval to support v2 JSON format

- Modified the `getCodexVersion` function to use the v2 JSON format for Homebrew cask information, improving compatibility with the latest Homebrew output.
- Updated unit tests to reflect changes in the expected JSON structure, ensuring accurate version retrieval and handling of installation states.

This change enhances the reliability of version detection for Codex installations via Homebrew.

docs: add community section with Telegram group links in multiple languages

- Introduced a new "Community" section in the README and documentation files for English, Japanese, and Chinese, encouraging users to join the Telegram group for support and discussions.
- Updated links to the official documentation site to reflect the new domain.

This update enhances user engagement by providing a platform for community interaction and support.

refactor(updater): improve update logic to support multiple installation methods

- Use Claude Code's built-in 'claude update' command instead of npm
  * Works for all installation methods (npm, Homebrew, manual)
  * Simplifies update logic by leveraging native command

- Implement smart installation method detection for Codex
  * Auto-detect npm vs Homebrew installation
  * Choose appropriate update command based on detection
  * Fallback to npm if installation method is unknown

- Add comprehensive i18n support for new features
  * Chinese translations for installation method messages
  * English translations with consistent terminology
  * User-friendly progress indicators

- Enhance code documentation
  * Add JSDoc comments for version checker functions
  * Clarify installation method agnostic behavior
  * Improve code readability and maintainability

This change improves cross-platform compatibility and provides better
user experience by automatically adapting to different installation methods.

refactor: update installMethod type and related functions for improved consistency

- Changed the installMethod type in ClaudeConfiguration to use the InstallMethod type from platform utilities, enhancing type safety.
- Updated the setInstallMethod function to remove the 'native' option and ensure it only accepts valid InstallMethod values.
- Adjusted the logic in getInstallMethodFromConfig and related functions to accommodate the new installMethod structure.
- Modified unit tests to reflect changes in installMethod handling, ensuring accurate configuration writes.

This update improves the clarity and consistency of installation method handling across the application.

feat: implement single API configuration saving to TOML

- Added functionality to save single API configurations to ZCF TOML for persistence and switching, enhancing user experience in managing API settings.
- Introduced new utility functions `saveSingleConfigToToml` and `convertSingleConfigToProfile` to handle profile creation and error reporting.
- Updated English and Chinese localization files to include success and failure messages for configuration saving.
- Added comprehensive unit tests to validate the new functionality and ensure robust error handling.

This update significantly improves the configuration management capabilities of the CLI, allowing users to easily save and switch between API profiles.

docs: simplify sidebar text by removing emojis for clarity

- Updated sidebar configuration in multiple language files to remove emojis from section titles, enhancing readability and consistency across the documentation.
- Adjusted the text for various sections including "Project Introduction," "Getting Started," "Features," and others to maintain a clean and professional appearance.

This update improves the overall presentation of the documentation, making it more accessible to users.

docs: add Halley-chan image and description to output styles documentation

- Included a new image and description for Tsundere Halley-chan in the pre-configured output styles sections across multiple language documentation files (English, Japanese, Chinese).
- Updated the assets directory to include the new Halley-chan image, enhancing the visual appeal and personality representation in the documentation.

This update enriches the documentation by providing a more engaging and relatable character for users.

fix: update installMethod to 'npm-global' for consistency in configuration

- Changed the installMethod type in ClaudeConfiguration and related functions to include 'npm-global' as a valid option.
- Updated the logic in getInstallMethodFromConfig and setInstallMethod to reflect the new install method.
- Adjusted unit tests to verify the correct handling of 'npm-global' in configuration writes.

This update ensures consistency in the installation method across the application, improving clarity for users.

feat: implement extra fields preservation in Codex MCP configuration

- Introduced support for preserving additional MCP configuration fields in Codex, allowing users to retain custom settings during updates.
- Enhanced `CodexMcpService` interface to include an `extraFields` property for storing non-standard fields.
- Updated `parseCodexConfig` and `renderCodexConfig` functions to handle extra fields, ensuring they are collected during parsing and correctly formatted during rendering.
- Added comprehensive unit and edge case tests to validate the functionality and ensure round-trip consistency for extra fields.
- Updated documentation to reflect the new feature and provide usage examples.

This update significantly improves the flexibility of Codex configurations, addressing user-reported issues with lost settings during updates.

chore: release v3.4.0

- Update version to 3.4.0
- Update CHANGELOG.md
- Generated by /zcf-release command

docs: update CLAUDE.md and installation documentation for versioning and installation methods

- Updated the version number in CLAUDE.md from v3.3.0 to v3.3.3 to reflect the latest release.
- Enhanced installation documentation to include a prompt for selecting the installation method for Claude Code, offering options like npm, homebrew, curl, and PowerShell.
- Improved clarity in the documentation by removing unnecessary icons from the Table of Contents in both English and Japanese versions.

This update ensures accurate versioning and provides users with clear installation options, enhancing the overall documentation quality.

feat: introduce path normalization utility for Windows compatibility

- Added a new function `normalizeTomlPath` to convert Windows backslashes to forward slashes, ensuring proper formatting in TOML files.
- Updated `getSystemRoot` and `normalizePath` functions to utilize the new normalization utility, reducing code duplication.
- Modified `renderCodexConfig` to normalize commands for consistency across configurations.

This update enhances cross-platform compatibility and improves the handling of Windows paths in the application.

feat: enhance internationalization support for provider management

- Removed hardcoded error messages in the provider manager and replaced them with internationalized keys for better localization.
- Added new keys for various provider management error messages in both English and Chinese, improving user experience for non-English speakers.
- Updated the provider management functions to utilize the new internationalization support, ensuring consistent messaging across different languages.

This update significantly improves the localization capabilities of the provider management features.

docs: add initial project documentation and metadata files

- Created context.md, patterns.md, preferences.md, and rules.md to provide essential project information and best practices.
- Added metadata.json to store project path, last organized date, total entries, and versioning details.
- Enhanced CLI setup by introducing a new option `--skip-prompt` for non-interactive mode, improving user experience during command execution.
- Updated unit tests to include checks for the new command options and their functionality.

This update establishes foundational documentation and improves CLI usability.

feat: enhance CLI uninstall and installation processes

- Added a new option `--code-type` to the uninstall command for selecting the code tool type (claude-code, codex).
- Updated the `installClaudeCode` and `installCodex` functions to support method selection, allowing users to choose their installation method (npm, homebrew, curl, etc.) interactively or skip selection.
- Improved error handling during uninstallation and installation processes, providing clearer feedback to users.
- Enhanced internationalization support by adding new keys for installation and uninstallation messages in English and Chinese.

This update improves user experience by providing more flexibility and clarity in managing code tool installations and uninstalls.

fix: update environment variable formatting in renderCodexConfig

- Changed the formatting of environment variable values in the renderCodexConfig function from double quotes to single quotes to avoid escaping issues with Windows paths.
- Added a new test case to verify that single quotes are used for environment variables, ensuring compatibility with Windows path formats and preserving backslashes without escaping.

This update enhances the handling of environment variables in the configuration rendering process.

docs: add acknowledgments section to README files

- Introduced an acknowledgments section in both README.md and README_ja-JP.md to recognize and thank the open-source projects that inspired this project.
- Included links to the respective projects, enhancing transparency and community engagement.

This update fosters appreciation for community contributions and encourages collaboration.

docs: update zcf-update-docs documentation for improved clarity and structure

- Revised the description to specify documentation updates in the `docs/` directory for multiple languages (en, zh-CN, ja-JP) and CLAUDE.md.
- Enhanced the context section to clarify the areas of documentation that require updates based on code changes.
- Updated the documentation structure reference to reflect the new organization of files and categories.
- Improved the validation checklist to ensure consistency across all language versions and documentation accuracy.

This update streamlines the documentation process, ensuring better alignment with code changes and enhancing user accessibility.

docs: streamline README files and update quick links to documentation

- Simplified content in README.md, README_zh-CN.md, and README_ja-JP.md by replacing quick links with direct links to the documentation site.
- Retained essential sections including sponsorship, logos, language switch, changelog, quick start, and footer information.
- Focused on providing a concise introduction and core quick start examples for better user accessibility.

ci: update documentation deployment workflow to trigger on main branch

- Changed the trigger branch for the documentation deployment workflow from 'docs' to 'main'.
This adjustment aligns the deployment process with the primary branch for better consistency.

releases.name: zcf@3.6.3
releases.body:
### Patch Changes

-   ## New Features

    -   Add BMAD multi-agent commands and workflows (bmad-agent-_, bmad-bmm-_, bmad-editorial-_, bmad-review-_, bmad-help, bmad-party-mode, etc.)
    -   Add Crazyrouter as a new sponsor in documentation and API provider presets

    ## 新功能

    -   新增 BMAD 多智能体命令与工作流（bmad-agent-_、bmad-bmm-_、bmad-editorial-_、bmad-review-_、bmad-help、bmad-party-mode 等）
    -   在文档与 API 提供商预设中新增 Crazyrouter 赞助商

    ## Documentation

    -   Update README (en, zh-CN, ja-JP) and docs with Crazyrouter sponsorship
    -   Add Crazyrouter to API providers documentation (en, zh-CN, ja-JP)

    ## 文档

    -   更新 README（中英日）及文档中的 Crazyrouter 赞助信息
    -   在 API 提供商文档（中英日）中新增 Crazyrouter

releases.name: zcf@3.6.2
releases.body:
### Patch Changes

-   ## New Features

    -   Upgrade bmad-init template from V4 to V6
    -   Allow skipping output style selection during init
    -   Update API provider presets and add new services

    ## 新功能

    -   将 bmad-init 模板从 V4 升级至 V6
    -   支持在初始化时跳过输出风格选择
    -   更新 API 提供商预设并新增服务

    ## Fixes

    -   Address Codex review feedback for codex-backup (skip tmp dir, handle dangling symlinks)
    -   Address Qodo/Cursor/Codex review feedback for output-style

    ## 修复

    -   根据 Codex 评审反馈改进 codex-backup（跳过 tmp 目录、处理悬空符号链接）
    -   根据 Qodo/Cursor/Codex 评审反馈改进 output-style

    ## Documentation

    -   Update sponsorship details in README and documentation files

    ## 文档

    -   更新 README 与文档中的赞助信息

releases.name: zcf@3.6.1
releases.body:
### Patch Changes

-   ## New Features

    -   Add AICodeMirror API provider presets for simplified configuration
    -   Support AICodeMirror as a new API provider option

    ## 新功能

    -   添加 AICodeMirror API 提供商预设，简化配置流程
    -   支持 AICodeMirror 作为新的 API 提供商选项

    ## Changes

    -   Remove Codex chat format support (deprecated feature)
    -   Simplify Codex configuration by removing chat format options

    ## 变更

    -   移除 Codex chat 格式支持（已弃用功能）
    -   简化 Codex 配置，移除 chat 格式选项

    ## Documentation

    -   Add AICodeMirror sponsorship details to README and documentation files
    -   Update API providers documentation with AICodeMirror information

    ## 文档

    -   在 README 和文档中添加 AICodeMirror 赞助信息
    -   更新 API 提供商文档，添加 AICodeMirror 相关信息

releases.name: zcf@3.6.0
releases.body:
### Minor Changes

-   ## New Features

    -   Add Leibus engineer output style with professional technical guidance
    -   Add rem-engineer output style for anime-inspired development assistance

    ## 新功能

    -   添加 Leibus 工程师输出风格，提供专业技术指导
    -   添加 rem-engineer 输出风格，提供动漫风格的开发辅助

    ## Fixes

    -   Enhance provider name validation in Codex API configuration

    ## 修复

    -   增强 Codex API 配置中的提供商名称验证

    ## Documentation

    -   Update output styles documentation for multiple languages (en, zh-CN, ja-JP)

    ## 文档

    -   更新多语言输出风格文档（英文、简体中文、日文）

releases.name: zcf@3.5.1
releases.body:
### Patch Changes

-   ## Fixes

    -   Implement targeted TOML updates to prevent MCP configuration corruption
    -   Improve newline handling and optional field insertion in zcf-config
    -   Enhance regex for top-level TOML fields to support inline comments
    -   Fix top-level field handling in updateTopLevelTomlFields

    ## 修复

    -   实现精准的 TOML 更新机制，防止 MCP 配置被破坏
    -   改进 zcf-config 中的换行处理和可选字段插入
    -   增强顶层 TOML 字段的正则表达式以支持行内注释
    -   修复 updateTopLevelTomlFields 中的顶层字段处理

    ## Features

    -   Update Codex model options and default configuration

    ## 新功能

    -   更新 Codex 模型选项和默认配置

    ## Refactor

    -   Replace smol-toml with @rainbowatcher/toml-edit-js for better TOML handling
    -   Remove writeCodexConfig function (code cleanup)

    ## 重构

    -   使用 @rainbowatcher/toml-edit-js 替换 smol-toml 以获得更好的 TOML 处理
    -   移除 writeCodexConfig 函数（代码清理）

    ## Documentation

    -   Move legacy plan markdown files to .zcf/plan/history
    -   Update sponsor sections in README files
    -   Fix output style command across multiple languages
    -   Update Haleclipse image and remove inline styles

    ## 文档

    -   将历史计划 markdown 文件移动到 .zcf/plan/history
    -   更新 README 文件中的赞助部分
    -   修复多语言中的输出样式命令
    -   更新 Haleclipse 图片并移除内联样式

readme_html:
✨ Full Documentation: ZCF Docs

Zero-config, one-click setup for Claude Code & Codex with bilingual support, intelligent agent system and personalized AI assistant

♥️ Sponsor AI API

This project is sponsored by Z.ai, supporting us with their GLM CODING PLAN. GLM CODING PLAN is a subscription service designed for AI coding, starting at just $10/month. It provides access to their flagship GLM-4.7 & （GLM-5 Only Available for Pro Users）model across 10+ popular AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cline, Roo Code, etc.), offering developers top-tier, fast, and stable coding experiences. Get 10% OFF GLM CODING PLAN：https://z.ai/subscribe?ic=8JVLJQFSKB

302.AI is a pay-as-you-go enterprise AI resource hub that offers the latest and most comprehensive AI models and APIs on the market, along with a variety of ready-to-use online AI applications.

🚀 Quick Start

  • Recommended: npx zcf opens the interactive menu and guides you through install/update.
  • Common commands:
npx zcf i        # Full initialization: install + workflows + API/CCR + MCP
npx zcf u        # Update workflows only
npx zcf --lang zh-CN  # Switch interface language (example)
  • Non-interactive example (provider preset):
npx zcf i -s -p 302ai -k "sk-xxx"

More usage, options, and workflows: see documentation.

📖 Full Documentation

  • https://zcf.ufomiao.com/

💬 Community

Join our Telegram group for support, discussions, and updates:

🙏 Acknowledgments

This project is inspired by and incorporates work from:

  • LINUX DO - New Ideal Community
  • CCR
  • CCometixLine
  • ccusage
  • BMad Method

Thanks to these community contributors for sharing their amazing projects!

❤️ Support & Sponsors

If you find this project helpful, please consider sponsoring its development. Your support is greatly appreciated!

Our Sponsors

A huge thank you to all our sponsors for their generous support!

【Corporate Sponsors】

  • 302.AI (first corporate sponsorship 🤠)

【Individual Sponsors】

  • Tc (first sponsor)

  • chamo101 (first GitHub issue sponsor 🎉)
  • 初屿贤 (first Codex user sponsor 🙅🏻‍♂️)

  • musistudio (first open source project author sponsor, the author of CCR 🤩)
  • BeatSeat (community expert 😎, provided $1000 Claude credits)
  • wenwen (community expert 🤓, provided daily $100 Claude&GPT credits)

Promotion Thanks
